title: 'The Andes virus is not as transmissible as other respiratory viruses'
subhead: Virologist Gustavo Palacios, one of the few experts to have worked on this type of outbreak, believes the risk of spread is limited if appropriate measures are taken. He said that infected people and their contacts must be strictly traced and symptomatic patients isolated.

title: Trump downplays threat to Iran ceasefire after exchange of attacks

UK PM says he 'takes responsibility' for Labour losses in local elections
subhead: By 8 am local time, Nigel Farage's anti-immigrant Reform UK had gained over 350 seats, while Labour under Keir Starmer had lost over 240 across 40 of the 136 English councils to announce results so far.

title: Leo XIV's return to realism and formalism at the Vatican
subhead: One year after his election as head of the Catholic Church, Pope Leo, Francis's successor, stands out from the Argentine pontiff in his approach, yet maintains a through line on substance.

title: France passes law on the restitution of cultural artifacts looted during colonial rule
subhead: The law, which fulfills a promise Emmanuel Macron made to a crowd of young African people in 2017, allows the government to approve the restitution of looted artifacts by decree, rather than requiring a specific law for each case.

title: 'Faced with Trump and Putin, the war in Ukraine is acting as a catalyst for Europe's autonomization'
subhead: Amid international turmoil, Donald Trump is punishing Germany, Ukraine is scoring points against Russia and Canada is emerging as Europe's staunchest supporter, observes Le Monde columnist Sylvie Kauffmann.

subhead: The defeat of outgoing prime minister Viktor Orban, who is set to hand over the reins of government to Péter Magyar on Saturday, is bringing a wave of change to the country. Oligarchs close to the former regime are now facing a surge of investigations.

title: Military personnel at French presidency suspected of compromising classified documents
subhead: Prosecutors are investigating several members of the military and civilians suspected of setting up a private company to sell classified information and weapons of war. Six suspects have been charged.

title: Le Pen condemns 'welfare dependency,' marking ideological about-face
subhead: In a speech on May 1, the far-right leader repeatedly used a term she had previously deemed discriminatory toward working-class people.
